seoTitle: How to Change the Meeting Organizer in Outlook
metaDescription: Outlook has no direct button to transfer meeting ownership, so learn the reply-all duplication trick and delegate access workarounds to reassign hosting.
intro: Outlook doesn't offer a single button to transfer the organizer tag on an existing meeting to someone else. This guide covers the practical workarounds instead: duplicating the invite through a reply-all draft, or granting delegate access so a teammate can manage the schedule going forward.

steps.heading: Locate the meeting to replicate
steps.body: Open your Outlook calendar and find the target meeting you want to reassign.

steps.heading: Reply all to the invite
steps.body: Click the Respond ribbon group and choose Reply All to pull in the existing distribution list.

steps.heading: Save it as a draft
steps.body: Save the reply as a draft and open it from your mail folder pane.

steps.heading: Convert it back into a meeting
steps.body: Use the Convert to Meeting ribbon command to reinject the invite as a fresh event.

steps.heading: Set the new details and send
steps.body: Confirm the calendar time, set the new organizer as the sender, and click Send.

steps.heading: Handle recurring meetings
steps.body: For recurring series, set an earlier end date on the original loop before launching the new series to avoid overlaps.

faq.question: Can I directly transfer meeting ownership in Outlook?
faq.answer: No, Outlook does not have a single button to transfer the organizer tag; instead you need to use a duplication workaround or delegate access.

faq.question: What is delegate access and how does it help?
faq.answer: Delegate access grants a teammate editor rights so they can modify scheduling on your behalf without needing to fully transfer ownership.

faq.question: Will attendees be confused by a duplicated invite?
faq.answer: It's a good idea to note in the new invite that it replaces the old one, since attendees will see it as a new meeting from a different organizer.

faq.question: What happens to the original recurring series?
faq.answer: You should set an earlier end date on the original recurring meeting to prevent it from overlapping with the newly organized series.
